findings.issue: Services layer at 19.88%, far below 99% gate. Every lib/services/implementations/*.dart file is at 1-30% (only Riverpod provider stubs hit during container resolution). No retry-exhausted, TTS-fallback, or protocol-contract tests exist; test-strategy §2.1 lists test/services/ as present (5+ files) but directory is absent from this repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Create test/services/ with per-service protocol + fake contract tests (sms_retry_exhausted_test.dart, audio_service_tts_fallback_test.dart, messaging_fan_out_test.dart, etc.); mock platform channels with MethodChannel.setMockMethodCallHandler.

findings.issue: Features layer at 18.11%. 11 of 13 feature controllers have 0% coverage (backup, battery_alert, contacts, distress_chains, fake_call, history, modes, profile, templates, plus near-zero onboarding/settings). Only session_controller (97.35%) and backup_service are tested. No widget tests exist.
findings.suggested_fix: Add controller tests using ProviderContainer.overrideWith and fake repositories for all 11 missing controllers, matching the session_controller_test pattern; add widget tests with pumpWithProviders helper.

findings.issue: Data layer at 36.03%. Generated DAO .g.dart boilerplate (20-30%) plus the encryption.dart (0%) and schema/tables.dart (0%) drag below 99% gate. No migration tests, no SQLCipher-key-missing/wrong-key recovery test (TEST-151/152), no schema-dump parity test (TEST-154), no concurrent-isolate serialization test (TEST-156).
findings.suggested_fix: Add test/data/db/ with encryption_test.dart (wrong-key rejection), schema_test.dart (committed schema dump parity), dao_round_trip_test.dart (parametrized over all tables). Exempt only .g.dart lines via explicit // COVERAGE comments per §9.2.

findings.issue: Models layer at 84.85%, below the 100% gate. 10 model files under 90%: chain_event (68%), session_context (46%), walk_session (74%), session_log (78%), step_config (83%), trigger (79%), user_profile (80%), mode_overrides (80%), gps_logging_config (79%), distress_chain (89%).
findings.suggested_fix: Extend model tests to cover: SessionContext message resolution (currently only 46%), WalkSession sealed-phase exhaustiveness, ChainEventData constructor branches, SessionLog serialization edge cases. Target: add ~40-60 missing tests.

findings.issue: Engine at 98.89% (3 uncovered lines in session_engine.dart, 1 in engine_state.dart). Below the 100% policy in §9.1.
findings.suggested_fix: Locate the 3 uncovered lines (likely defensive fall-through in state transitions or an unhandled sealed case) and add targeted tests or document exemption per §9.2.

findings.issue: Orchestration at 99.47% (1 uncovered line in event_strategy_registry.dart). Below the 100% policy.
findings.suggested_fix: Cover the single remaining branch in the registry (likely unknown-step-type fallthrough) or add §9.2 exemption.

findings.issue: Router coverage 53.85% vs 100% gate — no route-by-route resolve test; only integration coverage hits boot route. redirect branches and first-launch detection unexercised.
findings.suggested_fix: Add test/router/app_router_test.dart: parametrize over every RouteName, push via GoRouter, assert resolved screen type. Cover first-launch redirect to onboarding + stealth redirect branches.

findings.issue: Core widgets pin_keypad (4.55%), hold_to_trigger_button (0%), pin_entry_dialog (0%), guardian_angela_logo (2%). These are shared widgets exercised by PinEntryScreen/PinSetupScreen — no widget tests yet.
findings.suggested_fix: Add test/core/widgets/*_test.dart using WidgetTester with golden-toolkit multiScreenGolden. At minimum: PinKeypad keypress -> callback, HoldToTriggerButton hold/release timing, PinEntryDialog duress-PIN silent branch.

findings.issue: test-strategy §2.1 inventory claims 180 test files / 3,830 cases; actual repo has 57 files / 886 cases. The inventory is aspirational, not current. Many TEST-### cases from §5 are not implemented (property fuzz: only 1 file; no golden tests; no accessibility tests; no RTL layout tests; no localization parity tests; no crash-recovery; no simulation-silent rearm tests).
findings.suggested_fix: Either update §2.1 to reflect reality or land the missing suites; otherwise §9 coverage gates are unenforceable. Block merging until inventory matches actual.

findings.issue: sms_contact_strategy_test.dart asserts `harness.messaging.calls` contains strings like 'sendToAll:2'. The fake records the count instead of per-channel, per-contact call data — cannot distinguish 'sent SMS to A and B via WhatsApp' from 'sent SMS to A and B via SMS'. TEST-016 (MessageChannel fan-out) under-asserted.
findings.suggested_fix: Upgrade FakeMessagingService to record List<(contactId, channel, body)> tuples so per-channel fan-out can be asserted. Required for P0 distress-chain correctness.

findings.issue: simulationDescription is only checked for 'contains SIM prefix' and '3' — not for localization (TEST-014 'returns non-empty localized text'). English-only fallback will pass tests silently if localization breaks.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend strategy tests to call simulationDescription under each locale from pumpLocalizations helper; assert returned text differs across at least en/de/fa.

findings.issue: FixedRandom/fakeAsync discipline is strong in engine tests (108 FixedRandom usages across 16 files, 160 fakeAsync usages across 14 files). test/features/backup/ uses real PBKDF2 KDF — test runs take 40+ seconds (saw 00:39-01:02 for a single 'PIN round-trip' suite iterating ~60 times). Not a correctness issue but a CI-time smell.
findings.suggested_fix: Inject a test-only KDF with reduced iteration count for determinism tests; keep one full-strength iteration as a smoke test.

findings.issue: wiring_contract_test.dart group 'real and simulation pairs are distinct concrete types' (lines 218-337) asserts `runtimeType != runtimeType`. Since the providers return different classes by name, this test cannot fail unless the providers become identical — tautological-adjacent. Not harmful, but low information.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with stronger assertion: each simulation service has a per-method contract test that asserts side-effect is recorded, not executed (e.g., SimulationSmsService.send records but does not dispatch). This addresses TEST-010..012 directly.

quality_observations.fixed_random_discipline: Strong (108 usages across 16 files, primarily engine)
quality_observations.error_path_coverage: Strong for engine (25 throws-checks in edge_cases_test + 11 in speed_multiplier_test); weak outside engine (no controller error-path tests)
quality_observations.pattern_strategy_exhaustive: Yes — one test file per ChainStepType strategy (9/9)
quality_observations.harness_reuse: Strong — test_helpers.dart with step()/holdStep()/smsStep() factories + StrategyHarness in _strategy_harness.dart
quality_observations.baseline_count_antipattern: Not found (no spec_compliance_test.dart in this repo)
quality_observations.test_suite_is_stable: Yes — 886/886 passing on a cold run

open_questions: test-strategy.md §2.1 claims 180 files / 3,830 cases — is that a target or a stale inventory? The current 57 files / 886 cases suggests the spec is forward-looking; if so, the 99% coverage gate is not yet enforceable and should be staged per §10 (rebuild phases).

Should .g.dart Drift-generated files count against the layer coverage, or be split into their own bucket with 0% target (they contain mostly identifier strings and table descriptors)? Currently they pull lib/data/db/ down to 30%.

Service implementations under lib/services/implementations/ cannot run their platform-channel bodies in unit tests. Are MethodChannel mock tests sufficient, or does §9.1's 99% target for services require integration-test level coverage? Per §9.2 each such method needs a // COVERAGE exemption.
